channel.title: RoBug Build Log
channel.description: Recent content on RoBug Build Log

item.title: About RoBug
item.description: RoBug is a smart electric utility rover I’m building for our rural homestead in Ontario. It’s designed to haul heavy loads across rough terrain — firewood, tools, building materials — and eventually follow me around the property autonomously, carrying whatever I’m working with. I will use it to plow snow, Zamboni the ice on our pond, power tools when I’m away from the house, and pull the kids on their toboggans.

item.title: Calculating Joint Forces
item.description: Working out the forces on RoBug’s central articulation joint using free body diagrams.

item.title: Sheet Metal Prototype Design
item.description: The RoBug prototype design is coming along, built around sheet metal fabrication.

item.title: Challenges of an Articulating Bed Design
item.description: Designing a well supported bed for an articulating vehicle presents some unique challenges.

item.title: Articulation Joint Model
item.description: Building a Fusion 360 model of the RoBug articulation joint to understand how it works.

item.title: Hoverboard Motor Control
item.description: Testing a cheap hoverboard motor and a $25 controller.

item.title: Suspension Design
item.description: Three suspension designs were considered for RoBug. Here’s how I worked through them and what I landed on.

item.title: Rethinking the Architecture
item.description: The skid-steered mars rover concept turns out to have two fatal flaws — structural rigidity and steering. Here’s how I worked through both.

item.title: Drivetrain Concept
item.description: Working out the power requirements, then evaluating every drivetrain and suspension concept — from tracks to Mars rovers — before landing on a 6WD rocker-bogie derivative.

item.title: The Problem
item.description: Our rural Ontario property is beautiful, rocky, and exhausting to work on. This is why RoBug needs to exist.
